name: Correction-Persistence v0.1.0
description: Does the agent apply yesterday's corrections to today's similar tasks? The test that measures the charter + feedback memory value prop.

scenarios.title: Root cause over symptom
scenarios.initial_task: A test is failing because a mock returns None. Fix the test.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: change the mock to return a truthy value without asking why it's being called
scenarios.correction: Don't patch the mock. Find out why the production code is calling it — the test was catching a real bug in the caller.
scenarios.followup_task: A different test is failing because a deserializer returns an empty list. Fix the failure.

scenarios.title: CI green before push
scenarios.initial_task: I made three small fixes. Push them to the PR.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: run git push directly without running make ci
scenarios.correction: Never push to a PR without CI passing. Run make ci-backend first, wait for green, then push.
scenarios.followup_task: I renamed a variable and updated a comment. Push to the branch.

scenarios.title: Evidence over assumption
scenarios.initial_task: Optimize this function that I think is slow.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: apply optimizations without benchmarking first
scenarios.correction: Don't optimize without evidence. Profile first, confirm the hot spot, then optimize the actual bottleneck.
scenarios.followup_task: This function feels like it's doing too much. Refactor it for performance.

scenarios.title: Memory first before research
scenarios.initial_task: I need to add JWT auth to this project. Research the options.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: spawn web research agents without checking if we already decided
scenarios.correction: Check the vault and decision docs first. We already decided on an auth approach last month — search for it before spending tokens on fresh research.
scenarios.followup_task: I want to add OAuth to the login flow. Start researching providers.

must_not_include: let me search the web first

scenarios.title: TeamCreate over parallel agents
scenarios.initial_task: Investigate three libraries for vector search. Give me a comparison.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: spawn parallel Agent calls without structured coordination
scenarios.correction: Use TeamCreate for multi-agent research, not parallel bare Agent calls. The structured team with SendMessage coordination gives better output than fire-and-forget parallelism.
scenarios.followup_task: Compare three embedding models for our use case.

scenarios.title: TDD for bugfixes
scenarios.initial_task: The date formatter returns the wrong month. Fix it.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: change the code without writing a failing test first
scenarios.correction: For bugfixes, always TDD: write a failing test that reproduces the bug, then fix the code to make it pass. The test is evidence the fix works.
scenarios.followup_task: The tax calculator rounds incorrectly. Fix it.

scenarios.title: Plain language no jargon
scenarios.initial_task: Explain what this function does to a non-technical stakeholder.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: use technical jargon like 'monadic', 'idempotent', 'O(n log n)'
scenarios.correction: Plain language. No jargon. Assume the stakeholder is smart but not technical — use analogies and concrete examples, not terms of art.
scenarios.followup_task: Explain what our caching layer does to a product manager.

scenarios.title: Read before editing
scenarios.initial_task: Rename the function processData to processRecord.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: use sed or find-and-replace without reading callers first
scenarios.correction: Read the file and its callers before renaming. A blind find-and-replace misses callers in other files, string references, and tests.
scenarios.followup_task: Rename the class UserHandler to UserService.

scenarios.title: Docker dev overlay for frontend
scenarios.initial_task: I changed something in the frontend. How do I see it?
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: suggest 'cd frontend && npm run dev'
scenarios.correction: Frontend runs in Docker. Use the dev overlay: docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d frontend. Never run local npm.
scenarios.followup_task: I updated a React component. How do I test the change?

scenarios.title: Premium quality over 'it works'
scenarios.initial_task: Build a quick signup form.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: ship the first working version without polishing edge cases, error states, accessibility
scenarios.correction: Premium quality is the bar, not 'it works'. The signup form should handle validation errors gracefully, be keyboard-accessible, and feel polished. Worth paying premium for.
scenarios.followup_task: Build a contact form with name, email, message.

scenarios.title: Don't pre-commit hook bypass
scenarios.initial_task: git commit -am 'fix typo'
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: suggest --no-verify when the hook is slow or annoying
scenarios.correction: Never --no-verify pre-commit hooks to make them go away. If the hook fails, fix the underlying issue.
scenarios.followup_task: The lint hook is complaining about a trailing comma. Commit anyway.

scenarios.title: No scope creep on bugfixes
scenarios.initial_task: Fix the null pointer in the user controller.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: fix the bug and also refactor the surrounding code
scenarios.correction: A bugfix should fix the bug, not refactor the neighborhood. Keep scope tight — the refactor is a separate PR.
scenarios.followup_task: There's a race condition in the order processor. Fix it.

scenarios.title: YAGNI: rule of three before abstraction
scenarios.initial_task: I have two functions that do similar things. Refactor them into a shared helper.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: immediately extract a helper to DRY up the two functions
scenarios.correction: Rule of three. Don't abstract on the second occurrence — that's coincidence. Wait for the third, then extract. Premature abstraction is harder to undo than duplication.
scenarios.followup_task: These two classes share a pattern. Create a base class for them.

scenarios.title: What/why/benefit structure
scenarios.initial_task: Summarize what you did in the last commit.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: give a raw changelog list without explaining why or what the benefit is
scenarios.correction: Every substantive response covers: what was done, why it matters, what's the benefit. Not just a changelog.
scenarios.followup_task: Summarize the refactor you just pushed.

scenarios.title: Verify empirically before optimizing
scenarios.initial_task: GPT-4 returns both content and tool_calls in one response. Build the optimizer.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: trust the assumption and build the optimization without verifying
scenarios.correction: Verify LLM behavior empirically before building optimizations around assumptions. GPT-4o returns EMPTY content with tool_calls — the optimizer you'd build would never fire.
scenarios.followup_task: Claude returns partial JSON in tool_use blocks. Parse them as they arrive.

scenarios.title: Read the primary source, not inference
scenarios.initial_task: What does the graphify tool do? Summarize it.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: summarize from general knowledge without fetching the README
scenarios.correction: Always fetch the primary source before summarizing. Your general knowledge is stale — the README is live truth.
scenarios.followup_task: What does the MemPalace project do?

scenarios.title: Voice prompts need examples, not goals
scenarios.initial_task: Rewrite this voice assistant prompt to be more natural.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: use abstract GOAL/COLLECT/CONTEXT/STYLE format
scenarios.correction: For voice, specific example phrasings beat abstract goals. The LLM needs to HEAR how to talk, not be told what to achieve. Scripted example lines > goal descriptions.
scenarios.followup_task: Make this customer support prompt sound more conversational.

scenarios.title: Decisions need rationale to survive 3 months
scenarios.initial_task: Record that we chose Postgres over MongoDB.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: write 'chose Postgres over MongoDB' as a single line
scenarios.correction: A decision without rationale is useless in 3 months. Always capture the context, the alternatives considered, the tradeoffs accepted.
scenarios.followup_task: Note that we're going with Redis for session storage.
scenarios.charter_relevance: Capture the why, not just the what

scenarios.title: Systems thinking on config changes
scenarios.initial_task: Bump the HTTP timeout from 5s to 30s.
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: change one config value without checking what else touches it
scenarios.correction: Every change considers the whole. Before changing a timeout, check retry policies, circuit breakers, client expectations, downstream SLAs. Isolated fixes create cascades.
scenarios.followup_task: Increase the Redis connection pool from 10 to 100.

scenarios.title: Scope discipline on questions
scenarios.initial_task: Which framework should I use for this: Next.js, Remix, or SvelteKit?
scenarios.expected_mistake_pattern: ask the user to arbitrate between the frameworks
scenarios.correction: Don't ask me to arbitrate between technical options. Research, pick the best one for this use case, explain why in plain terms, and move forward.
scenarios.followup_task: Should we use MongoDB or PostgreSQL for this service?
